The issue is clear. Your root `package.json` declares `"packageManager": "pnpm@9.15.0"`, but Railway's default Node.js build environment doesn't have pnpm pre-installed. You have two options:

```toml
[phases.setup]
nixPkgs = ["nodejs_20", "corepack"]

[phases.install]
cmds = ["corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@9.15.0 --activate && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile"]

[phases.build]
cmds = ["pnpm --filter @synthesis-hack/web build"]

[start]
cmd = "pnpm --filter @synthesis-hack/web start"
```

**Option 2 — Add a Dockerfile for the web app (more control)**

Create `apps/web/Dockerfile` to fully control the build.

The fix is either:

The problem is that pnpm uses symlinks in `node_modules`, and those symlinks break when Docker `COPY`s them to the production stage. The runtime can't find the packages.

file.content:
FROM node:20-slim AS base

RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@9.15.0 --activate

WORKDIR /app

# Copy root workspace files
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml ./

# Copy only x402-services package
COPY packages/x402-services ./packages/x402-services

# Install dependencies for x402-services only
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --filter @synthesis-hack/x402-services...

# Build
RUN pnpm --filter @synthesis-hack/x402-services build

# --- Production stage ---
FROM node:20-slim AS production

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=base /app/packages/x402-services/dist ./dist
COPY --from=base /app/packages/x402-services/package.json ./
COPY --from=base /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=base /app/packages/x402-services/node_modules ./packages/x402-services/node_modules

ENV PORT=3002

EXPOSE 3002

CMD ["node", "dist/index.mjs"]
